sections.label: Pallavi
sections.ta:
வெண்ணை கையிருக்க நெய் தேடுவார் போல்
மண்ணை தின்ற மாயா உனையெங்கும் தேடி திரிந்தேன்
sections.translit:
veNNai kaiyirukka ney thEduvAr pOl
maNNai thindRa mAyA unaiyengum thEdi thirindhEn
sections.en: I was looking here and there for you Lord, you who gleefully ate soil. I was like the folks who look frantically for ghee while having butter in their own hands.

sections.label: Anupallavi
sections.ta:
ஓடி அலைகையிலே ருக்மணி லோலா ராஜகோபாலாவென
கோடி பேர் சொல்லியே கூவி அழைத்தேன்
sections.translit:
Odi alaigaiyilE rukmaNi lOlA rAjagOpAlAvena
kOdi pEr solliyE kUvi azhaitthEn
sections.en: While wandering and looking for you everywhere, I was yelling, calling you by a million names such as Rukmani’s beloved, Rajagopala.

sections.label: Charanam
sections.ta:
சோதை திருகியே சிவந்து கன்னிய காதொடு
போதை தரும் கோபியர் பூசிய கந்தமொடு
கட்டுக் கார் குழலொடு இடை நின்ற பட்டொடு
சுட்டு விழியொடு குயில் நாணும் குழலிசையொடு
விண்ணையளந்த நீ விளையாட்டாய் அருகிருந்து இமை சிமிட்ட
கண்ணை மறைத்ததின் பெயர் தானோ அஞ்ஞானம்
sections.translit:
sOdhai thirugiyE sivandhu kanniya kAdhodu
bOdhai tharum gOpiyar pUsiya ganthamodu
kattuk kAr kuzhalodu idai nindRa pattodu
suttu vizhiyodu kuyil nANum kuzhalisaiyodu
viNNaiyaLandha nI viLaiyAttAy arugirundhu imai simitta
kaNNai maRaitthadhin peyar dhAnO anyAnam
sections.en: Oh Lord, one who fathomed the entire sky with your feet, here you were standing right next to me. You were playfully blinking your eyes, your ears were red and sore because of Yasoda’s twists, with the fragrance of sandlewood paste applied by the intoxicating Gopi maidens. You have bundled dark hair and silk robes adorning your waist, mesmerizing shiny eyes, and the melodious rendering of flute that embarrassed even the most musical cuckoo birds. Is this what you call sheer ignorance, that my eyes did not see your presence in such close proximity? Author’s Note This song on Krishna rises from the poet's imagination, looking for the Lord everywhere and not realizing that the Mayan was in his proximity. The poet revels in using the names Rukmani Lola and Rajagopala, two of his favorite people and inspirational figures who he spent many years with. Rukmani mami used to be a violin teacher in the Bay Area and Rajagopala mama was active at the Hindu temple in Livermore, California. Both lived in Union City, California, in those enjoyable years.
